steps: Wash 5 confluent 10cm plates with 1xDPBS For the NGFR-mNG2 WM989 A6-G3 cell lines you can expect 4-5 million cells on a confluent 10cm dish. Scale up the number of starting plates accordingly.
Aspirate 1xDPBS and add 2mL 0.05% Trypsin-EDTA. Incubate at 37ºC for 3-5 minutes. undetermined, 0.05% Trypsin EDTA Undetermined, incubate 00:03:00, incubate

Neutralize trypsin with 4mL TU2% media. Transfer dissociated cells to 15mL falcon tube. undetermined, TU2% media Can use 1 15mL falcon tube for 2.5 10cm plates. Or scale down the volume of trypsin and media to fit more cells into fewer tubes.

Pellet cells by centrifugation at 1,000 rpm (200rcf) for 3 minutes. 00:03:00, Centrifugation During centrifugation, prepare sterile 1xDPBS with 2mM EDTA. Prepare excess. Alternatively, can centrifuge at 300 rcf
Resuspend cell pellet in 5mL 1xDPBS with 2mM EDTA. undetermined, 1xDPBS with 2mM EDTA Can combine pellets at this point

Pellet cells by centrifugation at 1,000 rpm (200rcf) for 3 minutes. 00:03:00, Centrifugation During centrifugation prepare 1xDPBS with 2mM EDTA and live dead staining solution. I use one of the following: 4uL/mL 7-AAD (final concentration 200ng/mL) 2uL/mL Propidium iodide (final concentration 100ng/mL) 1uL/mL DAPI (final concentration 50ng/mL). Prepare excess for diluting cells if necessary. During centrifugation prepare sterile 5mL polypropylene FACS tubes with 1mL TU2% for collecting sorted cells.
Resuspend cell pellets in 1xDPBS with 2mM EDTA and dead cell stain. I will typically use 6mL for 5-10cm plate's worth of cells.
Remove cell clumps by passing cell suspension through cell strainer cap into sterile 5mL polypropylene FACS tubes. Place tubes on ice.
Bring with you the following: 1xDPBS with 2mM EDTA and dead cell stain for diluting cells 5mL polypropylene FACS tubes with 1mL TU2% for collecting cells Excess FACS tubes and media
creator: Benjamin Emert
